title: Booking a table by phone
description: Call ahead to reserve a table — get the date, time and party size sorted
location: On the phone, anywhere in HK
context: Most HK restaurants take phone bookings, especially for groups of four or more. You'll need to give your name, party size, time, and sometimes a phone number. Polite phone Cantonese is slightly more formal than face-to-face.
culturalFact: In Hong Kong it's common to book under just a surname — 陳, 李, 王. Staff will address you as 陳生 (Mr Chan) or 陳小姐 (Miss Chan) throughout the call.

lines.speaker: staff
lines.jyutping: wai3, hou2 wan6 zau2 lau4, nei5 hou2.
lines.english: Hello, Good Fortune Restaurant, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 你好，我想訂枱，聽晚七點，三位。
lines.jyutping: nei5 hou2, ngo5 soeng2 deng6 toi2, ting1 maan5 cat1 dim2, saam1 wai2.
lines.english: Hi, I'd like to book a table — tomorrow evening at seven, three people.

lines.speaker: staff
lines.english: Sure, may I have your name?

lines.speaker: staff
lines.cjk: 陳生，聽晚七點，三位，幫你記低喇。有冇特別要求？
lines.jyutping: can4 saang1, ting1 maan5 cat1 dim2, saam1 wai2, bong1 nei5 gei3 dai1 laa3. jau5 mou5 dak6 bit6 jiu1 kau4?
lines.english: Mr Chan — tomorrow at seven, table for three, noted. Any special requests?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 有個細路仔，可唔可以要個BB椅？
lines.jyutping: jau5 go3 sai3 lou6 zai2, ho2 m4 ho2 ji5 jiu3 go3 BB ji2?
lines.english: We have a small child — could we get a high chair?

lines.speaker: staff
lines.jyutping: mou5 man6 tai4, ngo5 dei6 bong1 nei5 on1 paai4.
lines.english: No problem, we'll arrange that for you.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1 saai3! ting1 maan5 gin3.
lines.english: Thank you so much! See you tomorrow evening.
